Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Cornell University News Service records, [ca. 1920s-2000s].
Collection Number: 4-3-15

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Cornell University News Service records, [ca. 1920s-2000s].
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
4-3-15
Abstract:
Includes correspondence, news press releases, notes, publicity material, newspaper clippings, distribution lists, administrative files, photographs, biographies, and other records of events at Cornell.
Creator:
Cornell University. News Service
Cornell University. News Bureau
Cornell University. University Relations
Quanitities:
250 cubic feet.
7 volumes.
Language:
Collection material in English
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY

The Cornell University News Service was formerly known as the Office of Public Relations, Public Relations and Information, and the News Bureau. It currently reports to the Vice President for University Relations.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Includes correspondence, news press releases, notes, publicity material, newspaper clippings, distribution lists, administrative files, photographs, biographies, and other records of events at Cornell such as an Andrew Dickson White Commemorative Service, 1957; Cornell University Dinners; communism at Cornell; Olin Library Dedication, 1962; visit of Vice President Richard M. Nixon, 1956; installation of Cornell President Deane Waldo Malott; Festival of Contemporary Arts, 1950-1959; School of Industrial and Labor Relations dedication; Cornell Latin American Year, 1965-1966; International Student Conference, 1965; Cornell's centennial; and other events. Typescript and mimeographed press releases concerning faculty, students, and events at Cornell University. Audiotapes of radio programs and campus events, radio productions, movie films, and other records.
Photographs used by News Service and in Publications in the normal course of their operations. Contains photographs used by News Service throughout its history, spanning a wide range of subjects, which includes buildings, people, events, student activities, and other Cornell-relation miscellany.
NOTES

Includes collections 8-1-15, 4-3-111, 8-2-350, 8-2-565, 4-3-885, 8-2-885, 4-3-922, 8-1-1186, 8-1-1321, 8-1-1699.
Photographic assignments done by Jill Peltzman are located in 4-3-15 Box 63.
See also Cornell University Relations and Public Information Office audio visual records collection #4-3-875: http://resolver.library.cornell.edu/cgi-bin/EADresolver?id=RMA00875.
See also Cornell Clippings File, #13-8-3367: http://resolver.library.cornell.edu/cgi-bin/EADresolver?id=RMA03367.
See also Cornell University Council records collection #8-2-1359 for public information office records pertaining to the Council and information pertaining to special campus events attended by members, such as dedications.
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Cornell University News Service records and add the appropriate call number either #4-3-15, #4-3-111, #4-3-885, #8-2-885, #8-1-1186 or #8-1-1699. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Conditions Governing Use

Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Audio visual material that has been digitized: TR-7026, TR-7210-TR-7211, TR-7219, TR-7223, TR-7228, TR-7243, TR-7247, TR-7254, TR-7257, TR-7269, TR-7275, TR-7286-TR-7290, TR-7312, TR-7325-TR-7327

Cornell University -- : Public relations.
Cornell University -- : Students.
Form and Genre Terms:
Photographs.

Series VI. Cornell University News Services records
 1920s-2000s
Press Releases, News Clippings, Distribution Lists, Administrative Files, Subject Files
Scope and Contents
This section of press releases is mostly unordered, but there are several subseries groups that remain together. Some sections are in alphabetical order and some sections have a roughly chronological order. A section of individuals by last name is beneath the general section.
General

Volume 8-1-15|22
Commencement and Reunion alumni registers
 1894-1907
Scope and Contents
These two ledgers were removed from collection and cataloged individually as 41-5-3978Guestbooks include the signature of guests, where they live, where they were staying on campus during the week, and their class year. The books are filled with many important names from Cornell's early graduates and faculty. Names of graduates go back to some of the earliest Cornell classes, including the Class of 1870. Notable names include Davy Hoy, Simon Henry Gage, Robert Henry Treman, Frank A Barton, Sao-Ke Alfred Sze, Louis Agassiz Fuertes, Clark Sutherland Northup, and many others.

Series VII. Cornell University News Service records, Audio and Visual Material
Scope and Contents
An extensive amount of tape recordings and movie films exists as part of this collection. Paper lists may exist, but not all the items have been listed online at this point.
A partial list of the tapes can be found here: http://rmc.library.cornell.edu/EAD/pdf_guides/Tape_Recording_Inventory.pdf
